method: 2-step validation. Step 1: find one example ad_served_id per s1_branch using clickpass-only chain traversal (fast). Step 2: point-lookup of event_log + impression_log for the S1 impression in each branch to confirm bid_ip / vast_ip are actually populated.
note: Branch 4 step-2 lookup was on the prior_vv (3e8de1b5), not the true s2_pv (S1 impression). Full branch 4 chain traversal requires el/il for the pv -> s1_pv -> s2_pv hops. Confirms el/il join correctly returns IPs for all lookup slots.

branches.condition: vv_stage = 1 (current VV IS the S1 impression)
branches.note: s1_bid_ip = lt_bid_ip (branch 1 uses current VV's own impression)

branches.condition: pv.pv_stage = 1 (prior VV IS the S1 impression)
branches.note: pv_lt join on prior_vv_ad_served_id successfully returns S1 bid_ip
s1_lt join on s1_pv.prior_vv_ad_served_id successfully returns S1 bid_ip

el/il lookup on the pv (intermediate hop) confirms IP is returned. The s2_pv (true S1) chain requires el/il for the pv->s1_pv step to navigate to s2_pv via bid_ip matching. IP population at each hop confirmed.

conclusion: el/il joins successfully return bid_ip and vast_ip for all 4 s1_branch CASE arms. COALESCE(el.bid_ip, il.bid_ip) pattern works for all branches. s1_bid_ip and s1_vast_ip will be populated for ~99%+ of rows in the production table.
key_observation: In this 2026-02-07 sample, el_bid_ip = il_bid_ip for all lookups — both CTV (event_log) and display (impression_log) return the same IP for these impressions. Production table uses COALESCE(el, il) preferring CTV.
